title: Regulation-Aware Sequence Decoder Over Rowwise Scores
summary: Pit calls are sequential decisions, not independent rows.
rationale: Pit calls are sequential decisions, not independent rows. FIA dry-race rules require two dry compounds unless wets/inters are used, and sequence models such as CRFs/HMM-style decoders are designed to score whole label paths with transition constraints. A post-model decoder can suppress impossible streaks and upweight plausible 'pit-window just opened' transitions without retraining the base learner.
implementation_hint: Train the usual rowwise model, convert OOF probabilities to logits, then decode each `(Race, Year, Driver)` sequence with a small dynamic program. Add transition penalties for consecutive `PitNextLap=1`, bonuses when compound-debt remains in dry races, and reset logic after actual `PitStop=1` / `TyreLife` reset. Keep all transition parameters tuned only on OOF folds.
expected_effect: Should clean up noisy isolated positives/negatives near stop boundaries and improve leaderboard robustness more than CV if current model is over-triggering around adjacent laps.
risk: Over-constrained decoding can hurt races with unusual safety-car or wet transitions; must gate rules when `Compound` is `INTERMEDIATE`/`WET` or the sequence already shows wet running.
